instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
Do You Need Term Insurance If the Plan Provided by Your Employer Is Sufficient?
A term insurance policy is a valuable addition to your portfolio because of the financial security that it offers. The plan covers the risk of premature death and secures the financial future of your family. In your absence, your family gets a financial benefit from the term plan that helps them meet their lifestyle expenses and financial goals.
Given the importance of term insurance, many employers offer coverage as a part of their employee welfare scheme. If you are a salaried employee and your employer offers a group term insurance plan, you can enjoy term insurance coverage under the group policy. Let’s understand what the policy is all about.
What is group term insurance?
A group term insurance coverage is a term plan offered to a collective group of people, like a group of employees, for instance. The plan allows coverage against the premature death of the employee. In the case of the employee’s death, his family receives the sum assured as the death benefit and the plan continues for the other covered employees.
The group term plan is bought by different types of groups to provide financial security to their members. One policy is issued, which covers all the named members for specified sum assured levels. The coverage is allowed for a specific tenure. After the tenure expires, the group can renew the coverage again.
Is your employer-sponsored group cover sufficient?
Your employer might invest in a group term cover for its employees, and if you are an employee, you get insured under the plan. The group term plan might offer a high sum assured for your financial needs. But is it sufficient? Let’s find out.
The sum assured is restricted:
Under group-term insurance plans, the sum assured is usually restricted. While your employer can provide a high coverage level, it might not be sufficient to cover all of your financial goals.
As such, you need an independent term insurance plan to supplement the coverage offered by your employer. An independent term plan would help you enhance the coverage at affordable premiums to ensure that your family is sufficiently compensated in your absence.
Coverage is contingent on continued employment:
The employer-sponsored group term plan is available only till you remain employed with your employer. If you switch jobs, you lose coverage. Moreover, the new employer might not offer the coverage in which case you would end up without any coverage at all.
Even when you retire from employment, the coverage stops. If you have pending financial liabilities, you might struggle to find a new term policy at an older age.
That’s why you stay protected even after retirement when you buy an independent term plan which allows coverage till older ages or even lifelong.
Term riders might be missing:
Riders are additional features that enhance the scope of coverage. You can get additional protection against accidental deaths, disablements and even critical illnesses through riders. Moreover, the term rider gives additional death benefits on premature demise.
Your employer-sponsored coverage might lack these riders, which limits the scope of coverage.
The tenure is not guaranteed:
The group coverage runs till the employer chooses to continue the coverage. If the employer opts out of the scheme, you lose coverage. Since a long-term term plan is better for longer coverage, the employer’s policy might fall short.
What’s the solution?
The solution has independent term insurance coverage. here’s why –
You can choose a sum assured based on your family’s financial needs and goals. You can, thus, get control over the coverage level and ensure that the term plan is adequate for your family’s future needs.
You can continue the coverage for as long as you want. You can choose the policy tenure, and it would remain unaffected even if you quit employment.
You can choose suitable riders, including the term rider, depending on your coverage needs. This allows you to customise your term life insurance policy, a feature that is not available under group term plans.
If you continue the premium payment as stipulated, you can enjoy coverage through the chosen tenure.
The bottom line
If you have group term insurance coverage, you can enjoy good coverage under the plan. However, the coverage might not prove sufficient for your financial needs. Adding an independent term insurance plan would be an ideal choice. You would be able to customise the plan per your coverage needs and enjoy continued coverage even when you switch jobs.
So, buy a suitable and comprehensive term life insurance plan along with the group policy for all-around protection.
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. No.: 116, CIN: U66010PN2001PLC015959, Call us on toll free No.: 1800 209 7272, Mail us: customercare@bajajallianz.co.in, Fax No: 02066026789
